name: DateRangePicker

unions.name: State
unions.comment:
DateRangePicker state.

Use helpers to set or retrieve values out of it.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
DateRangePicker configuration:

  - `allowFuture`: Allow picking a range in the future
  - `applyRangeImmediately`: Apply predefined range immediately when clicked
  - `weekdayFormatter`: How to format a [`Time.Weekday`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/time/latest/Time#weeks-and-months)
  - `monthFormatter`: How to format a [`Time.Month`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/time/latest/Time#weeks-and-months)
  - `noRangeCaption`: The String to render when no range is set
  - `weeksStartOn`: The [`Time.Weekday`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/time/latest/Time#weeks-and-months) weeks start on (eg. `Time.Mon` or `Time.Sun`)
aliases.type: { allowFuture : Basics.Bool, applyRangeImmediately : Basics.Bool, weekdayFormatter : Time.Weekday -> String.String, monthFormatter : Time.Month -> String.String, noRangeCaption : String.String, weeksStartOn : Time.Weekday }

values.name: configure
values.comment:
Helper for selectively altering defaultConfig:

    configure (\default -> { default | weeksStartOn = Time.Sun })
        |> init Nothing

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment:
A Config featuring the following default values:

    { allowFuture = True
    , applyRangeImmediately = True
    , weekdayFormatter = Helpers.weekdayToString
    , monthFormatter = Helpers.monthToString
    , noRangeCaption = "N/A"
    , weeksStartOn = Time.Mon
    }

values.name: disable
values.comment: Disable or enable a DateRangePicker.

values.name: getValue
values.comment: Get the current DateRangePicker.Range of the DateRangePicker, if any.

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initializes a State with the current day represented as a Posix.

    import Time

    init defaultConfig Nothing (Time.millisToPosix 0)

values.name: initToday
values.comment:
Initializes a State using a Task for fetching today's date.

    import Task

    type Msg
      = DateRangePickerCreated State

    initToday defaultConfig Nothing
      |> Task.perform DateRangePickerCreated
values.type: DateRangePicker.Config -> Maybe.Maybe DateRangePicker.Range.Range -> Task.Task Basics.Never DateRangePicker.State

values.name: isDisabled
values.comment: Checks if the DateRangePicker is currently disabled.

values.name: isOpened
values.comment: Checks if the DateRangePicker is currently opened.

values.name: open
values.comment: Open or close a DateRangePicker.

values.name: panel
values.comment:
A DateRangePicker panel view.

The panel is the content of the window containing the ranges and the two calendars.
This is useful when you don't need to deal with the clickable date input.

Usage is similar to [`view`](#view).

values.name: setRange
subscriptions
values.comment:
DateRangePicker subscriptions. They're useful if you want an opened DateRangePicker
panel to be closed when clicking outside of it.

values.name: view
values.comment:
The main DateRangePicker view.

If you only need the panel content, have a look at [`panel`](#panel).

name: DateRangePicker.Range
comment:
Date range management.

@docs Range, between, days, decode, encode, format, fromString, toString

values.name: between
values.comment:
Checks if a [`Time.Posix`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/time/latest/TimePosix)
is comprised within a [`Range`](#Range).

values.name: days
values.comment: Computes the number of days in a [`Range`](#Range).

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decodes a [`Range`](#Range) from JSON.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes a [`Range`](#Range) to JSON.

values.name: format
values.comment: Formats a [`Range`](#Range) in simple fashion.

values.name: fromString
values.comment:
Extract a [`Range`](#Range) from a String, where the two Posix timestamps are
encoded as UTC to Iso8601 format and joined with a `;` character.

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Turns a [`Range`](#Range) into a String, where the two Posix timestamps are
encoded as UTC to Iso8601 format and joined with a `;` character.
